$comment: Source registry for mcc.vesperio.ai ingestion agent. status: 'verified' = URL/feed confirmed working; 'unverified' = drafted from knowledge, must be checked before first run; 'dead' after 3 consecutive failures. tier is legacy metadata (1 = primary-leaning, 2 = press); the SNR model classes each attached source per claim (see CLAUDE.md 'The SNR score' and prompts/update-items.md). Signal-tier sourcing is governed by signals.json, not this file.

launch_providers.name: SpaceX
launch_providers.notes: Sparse official comms. Launch cadence better tracked via Launch Library API. | [2026-07-05] Standard fetch returned only the page header (JS-rendered, no article content) on two attempts; needs a JS-capable fetch or an alternate feed. | [2026-07-05] Fetched again (both WebFetch and curl with descriptive User-Agent); still only the Angular app shell with no article markup, no content extractable. One more consecutive failure flips this to dead. | [2026-07-05] Third consecutive failure. Both WebFetch and curl with a descriptive User-Agent return only the Angular app shell (no article markup); flipping to dead per the 3-failure rule. Re-verify manually if the site changes to a server-rendered news page.

launch_providers.name: Rocket Lab
launch_providers.notes: Also SEC (RKLB). Domain changed from rocketlabusa.com in 2025, verify. | [2026-07-05] HTTP 403 Forbidden on two attempts; site may be blocking the fetch tool's user agent. | [2026-07-05] The /updates/ listing page now fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent (real headlines and dates), reversing the prior 403. Individual article pages, however, are gated by a Cloudflare JS challenge (403 via both WebFetch and curl) -- listing metadata alone isn't enough to verify facts for publication; see held item. | [2026-07-05] The /updates/ listing page fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent (same behavior as last run). Individual article pages are still gated by a Cloudflare JS challenge, but the Rocket Lab/Iridium deal referenced in the listing was independently confirmed this run via Rocket Lab's own 8-K exhibit on SEC EDGAR, so the listing-vs-article gap no longer blocks that story. | [2026-07-05] The /updates/ listing again fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent and lists the VICTUS HAZE Puma and Synspective StriX-10 launches, but individual article pages remain Cloudflare-gated (same as prior runs). VICTUS HAZE was independently confirmed via Launch Library's mission description instead. | [2026-07-05] The /updates/ listing fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent; most recent entry still the July 3 Iridium acquisition post (already published from the RKLB 8-K). No new dated entries since. | [2026-07-06] The /updates/ listing fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent; most recent entry still the July 3 Iridium acquisition post (already published). No new dated entries. | [2026-07-06] [2026-07-06] The /updates/ listing fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent; most recent entry still the July 3 Iridium acquisition post (already published). No new dated entries since last sweep.

launch_tracking.name: Launch Library 2 (TheSpaceDevs)
launch_tracking.notes: Free API, rate-limited (~15 req/hr unauth). The backbone for launch events. Verify current API version. | [2026-07-05] Fetched previous/upcoming launches directly; no SpaceX or Rocket Lab launch within the freshness window carried a notable, sourced angle beyond the routine manifest. | [2026-07-05] One previous + one upcoming call; no SpaceX or Rocket Lab launch in the window carried a notable, sourced angle beyond the routine manifest. | [2026-07-05] One previous + one upcoming call (15 results each), consistent with the rate-limit lesson. SpaceX and Rocket Lab entries are routine Starlink/Electron manifest launches; none carried a notable, sourced angle beyond the routine cadence already on record. | [2026-07-05] One previous-launches call plus one direct per-launch fetch for VICTUS HAZE; used to confirm launch occurrence facts for VICTUS HAZE (confirmed tier) and to cross-check the Atlas V/Amazon Leo mission. | [2026-07-05] One previous-launches call (15 results). The one launch inside the freshness window (Falcon 9 Starlink Group 10-50, 2026-07-05 10:50 UTC) is routine cadence with no notable angle beyond record; no candidate. | [2026-07-06] One previous-launches call (2.3.0, 15 results) plus one upcoming call; no launch inside the freshness window (last sweep 2026-07-05 18:12 UTC) carried a notable, sourced angle beyond the routine manifest already on record. | [2026-07-06] [2026-07-06] One previous-launches call (2.2.0, 10 results) plus one upcoming call; most recent previous launch is 2026-07-05T13:43 UTC (Long March 8A/SpaceSail Polar Group #14), predating this run's freshness window. No candidate. | [2026-07-06] 2.3.0 previous-launch list endpoint still 404s (consistent with prior notes); 2.2.0 previous-launch list works via curl. Used to confirm launch-occurrence facts and cross-check cadence for SpaceSail Polar Group #13/#14, Haiyang 2E, Sirius SXM-11, the Atlas V/Amazon Leo finale, and the Pegasus/Swift mission; none of the routine constellation/marine-satellite launches carried a notable angle beyond record.

launch_tracking.name: Gunter's Space Page
launch_tracking.notes: Best free satellite/payload encyclopedia. Registry reference source, never a news basis. Terms: summarization/RAG only with clear attribution and a direct link to the exact page; cite deep-link URLs in registry source fields, never sum counts across pages.

regulatory.name: FCC IBFS (satellite filings)
regulatory.notes: fcc.report is an unofficial but scrapeable mirror; official IBFS search is licensing.fcc.gov and painful. Constellation modifications show up here months before press releases. Differentiator source. | [2026-07-06] First fetch of fcc.report/IBFS/ this project: loads cleanly (200) including the Filing-List.rss feed and the SAT/ filing-type sublist, but every listed filing across all three views is dated 2020-2023 despite the RSS channel's own pubDate claiming today. This mirror appears to serve a static/stale snapshot rather than live IBFS data; not usable for current-filing discovery as configured. No candidate.

regulatory.name: FCC Daily Digest
regulatory.notes: [2026-07-06] curl (descriptive User-Agent, 20s timeout) timed out with no response, consistent with fcc.gov being blocked from this fetch environment before. First documented failure for this source; not yet flipping to dead (needs 3 consecutive).

regulatory.name: ITU Space Network filings (SNL)
regulatory.notes: Clunky. Treat as phase-2 hardening target, not day-one. | [2026-07-06] www.itu.int/ITU-R/space/snl/ 301-redirects to a WordPress 'Space Networks Regulatory Hub' portal page that fetches cleanly. It is a landing/tools hub (links to online lookup tools and reports), not a dated filing list, so no filings were extractable this way. Matches the existing note that this source is clunky; first clean fetch, flipped to verified. No candidate.

regulatory.name: NOAA CRSRA (US remote sensing licenses)
regulatory.notes: License grants/modifications reveal US EO operator capability plans early (resolution, NEI waivers). URL may have moved; confirm during recon. | [2026-07-06] Configured URL (nesdis.noaa.gov/about/commercial-remote-sensing-regulatory-affairs) now 404s. nesdis.noaa.gov/CRSRA 301-redirects cleanly to space.commerce.gov's Commercial Remote Sensing Regulatory Affairs pages (Office of Space Commerce). Checked the Mission Authorization news category there; most recent post is April 2, 2026, predating this run's window (2026-06-29). No dated license-grant log found, only static regulatory-info pages. Recommend updating the stored URL to nesdis.noaa.gov/CRSRA. No candidate.

procurement.name: SAM.gov (space/EO NAICS filters)
procurement.notes: Free API with key. Filter by NAICS 517410/336414 and keyword sets. Phase in after core news loop works. | [2026-07-06] sam.gov/search/ returns only an unrendered Angular app shell (200, no listing data in the fetched HTML); the source's own notes say it needs the free API with a key, which this run does not have. No candidate; needs the keyed API path to be usable.

procurement.name: ESA Open Invitations (esa-star)
procurement.notes: [2026-07-06] esastar-publication-ext.sso.esa.int/ returns only an unrendered Angular app shell (200, no listing data in the fetched HTML), same failure mode as SAM.gov. No candidate.

procurement.name: EUSPA procurement
procurement.notes: [2026-07-06] Configured URL (euspa.europa.eu/opportunities/procurement) 301-redirects to opportunities/procurement-grants/procurement, which fetches cleanly and lists ongoing/planned procurements. Checked the Space Traffic Coordination Platform (STCP) planned-procurement entry specifically; its post date is 16 March 2026, predating this run's window. No procurement inside the 2026-06-29 window. Recommend updating the stored URL to the post-redirect path.

procurement.name: NGA / NRO public announcements
procurement.notes: Commercial imagery contract news (EOCL-type). High relevance to EO revenue picture. | [2026-07-06] Configured URL (nga.mil/news/press_releases.html) 404s. nga.mil/news/ resolves via a client-side redirect to news/News.html, which links a Contract_Announcements.html page that fetches cleanly and lists dated commercial-imagery contract awards (Maxar, BlackSky, Planet Labs Federal all appear in the Luno B IDIQ, Jan 15 2025). Most recent entry is December 31, 2025 (GDTS IV to Raytheon), predating this run's window. Recommend updating the stored URL to nga.mil/news/Contract_Announcements.html. No candidate.

non_english.name: CASC news (China Aerospace)
non_english.notes: EN version thin; zh original at spacechina.com richer. Chinese launch cadence is the biggest blind spot in Western feeds. Differentiator. | [2026-07-06] First clean fetch this run at english.spacechina.com; three dated, linked headlines (Long March-8A and Long March-6A SpaceSail constellation launches, Long March-4B Haiyang 2E marine satellite), all routine cadence already confirmed via Launch Library and not itemized separately. Flipped from unverified to verified.

non_english.name: CGSTL / Chang Guang
non_english.notes: Jilin-1 constellation. Validated as OSINT-rich during planning research. WeChat is the richer channel but not machine-accessible; treat site-only for v1.

non_english.name: CNES press (FR)
non_english.notes: You read FR natively; agent handles it fine too. | [2026-07-06] Configured URL (presse.cnes.fr/fr) 301-redirects to cnes.fr/presse; fetched cleanly there. Most recent release (Sirius Space at Diamant, June 18) predates this run's freshness window; no candidate. Flipped from unverified to verified; worth updating the stored URL to the post-redirect one.

non_english.name: DLR press (DE)
non_english.notes: [2026-07-06] Both the configured URL and an /en/latest/news guess return only a client-side-rendered "Loading" shell with no headlines in the fetched HTML, on both WebFetch and curl. Needs an RSS/JSON endpoint or a different fetch approach to be usable; first documented failure, not yet flipped to dead.

non_english.name: Xinhua tech/space (EN)
non_english.notes: Fallback when CGSTL/CASC sites are unreachable from outside China. State media: label origin on items, treat launch facts as reliable, framing as not. | [2026-07-06] Configured URL (english.news.cn/tech/index.htm) 404s; the bare homepage (english.news.cn) loads and surfaces space headlines instead. Both space headlines visible this run (Tianwen-2 arrival, Long March-8A SpaceSail launch) were already covered via CASC and Launch Library and evaluated (excluded/routine). Source URL likely needs correcting to the homepage or an /indepth section path.

aggregators_crosscheck.name: SpaceNews
aggregators_crosscheck.notes: Discovery + cross-check only. Never republish their reporting as an item basis; find the primary source they cite. | [2026-07-05] First successful fetch of the RSS feed. One entry inside the freshness window (Airbus/ESA Aeolus-2 development contract, July 5 15:17 UTC) evaluated and excluded as out of scope: a legacy ESA/Eumetsat institutional weather-science procurement with Airbus as heritage prime, not a new-space-economy event. See SWEEP_MEMORY for the judgment call. | [2026-07-06] RSS feed fetched cleanly. One entry inside the freshness window (NASA/SBA strategic capital partnership, July 6 01:43 UTC) used as the reported-tier basis for a new item; the Airbus/Aeolus-2 entry (July 5 15:17 UTC) predates the window and was already excluded last run. | [2026-07-06] [2026-07-06] RSS feed fetched cleanly. Three entries inside the freshness window (last sweep 09:07:12 UTC): Tianwen-2 asteroid arrival (09:14 UTC, excluded as deep-space science with no commercial-provider angle), a Centre for Space Futures/Novaspace/SpaceTech Gulf memorandum to build a space-capability mapping dashboard (10:00 UTC, excluded as an institutional analytics/mapping initiative rather than a commercial-market-moving event), and a Novaspace in-flight-connectivity market forecast report (12:00 UTC, excluded as third-party growth-analytics/market research, not a discrete actor event). No candidate shipped. | [2026-07-06] RSS feed fetched cleanly; 24 entries inside the freshness window, used as the trade-tier basis for six new items plus corroboration on three existing items.
